name: Jelebi (Indian Fried Dough)
ingredients: Rose water or cardamom powder for flavor (optional)

steps.title: Mix Dry Ingredients
steps.instruction: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together flour, salt, baking soda, and baking powder.
steps.why: To ensure yeast-free leavening and structure in the dough.
steps.tip: Use room temperature ingredients for better mixing.

steps.title: Create Wet Ingredients
steps.instruction: In a separate bowl, whisk together milk, eggs, and ghee or vegetable oil.
steps.why: To create a smooth and creamy starter for the dough.
steps.tip: Use full-fat milk for a more authentic taste.

steps.title: Combine Wet and Dry Ingredients
steps.instruction: Slowly pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until a smooth batter forms.
steps.why: To distribute the liquid evenly and create a delicate dough.
steps.tip: Don't overmix the batter, as it will lead to dense lembas.

steps.title: Rest the Dough
steps.instruction: Let the batter rest for 1 hour in a warm, draft-free place, allowing it to ferment.
steps.why: To allow the yeast-free leavening agents to activate and the dough to rise.
steps.tip: Don't disturb the dough during this period.

steps.title: Heat the Oil
steps.instruction: Heat sufficient vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat.
steps.why: To create a hot, even layer of oil for frying the lembas.
steps.tip: Monitor the oil temperature to prevent it from getting too hot.

steps.title: Fry the Jelebis
steps.instruction: Using a piping bag or a spoon, drop small portions of the rested dough into the hot oil, about 1-inch apart.
steps.why: To cook the lembas evenly and prevent them from sticking together.
steps.tip: Don't overcrowd the pan, as it may lead to steaming instead of frying.

steps.title: Drain and Serve
steps.instruction: Remove the fried jelebis from the oil with a slotted spoon and place them on paper towels to drain excess oil.
steps.why: To remove excess oil and prevent oil absorption.
steps.tip: Serve the jelebis hot or at room temperature, as desired.

steps.title: Optional: Add Flavor and Sugar
steps.instruction: Dust the jelebis with powdered sugar or rose water and cardamom powder, if desired.
steps.why: To enhance the flavor and texture of the lembas.
steps.tip: Use a light hand when dusting the jelebis, as the flavor should be subtle.

steps.title: Preheat Oven and Unfold Puff Pastry
steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
steps.why: This step ensures the pastry cooks evenly.
steps.tip: Use cold puff pastry for best results.

steps.title: Prepare Egg Mixture
steps.instruction: In a bowl, whisk together the eggs, grated cheddar cheese, chopped scallions, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
steps.why: This step combines the flavors and textures of the filling.
steps.tip: Use room-temperature eggs for easier whisking.

steps.title: Brush the Pastry with Egg Mixture
steps.instruction: Brush the entire surface of the pastry with the egg mixture, leaving a small border around the edges.
steps.why: This step adds flavor and helps hold the pastry together.
steps.tip: Don't overfill the pastry, or it will be difficult to seal.

steps.title: Fold the Pastry and Seal the Edges
steps.instruction: Fold the pastry in half, lengthwise, and press the edges together to seal. Use a fork to crimp the edges.
steps.why: This step creates the puffed shape and keeps the filling inside.
steps.tip: Make sure the edges are tightly sealed to prevent filling leakage.

steps.title: Bake the Eggpuff
steps.instruction: Place the eggpuff on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 15 minutes.
steps.why: This step cooks the pastry and puffs it to the correct size.
steps.tip: Don't overbake, or the pastry will become too dark.

steps.title: Increase Oven Temperature and Finish Baking
steps.instruction: Increase the oven temperature to 425°F (220°C) and bake for an additional 10-12 minutes, or until the eggpuff is golden brown.
steps.why: This step finishes cooking the eggpuff and adds color to the pastry.
steps.tip: Keep an eye on the eggpuff during this time, as oven temperatures can vary.

steps.title: Check for Doneness
steps.instruction: Check the eggpuff for doneness by gently pressing on the pastry. It should be firm and golden brown.
steps.why: This step ensures the eggpuff is cooked through and safe to eat.
steps.tip: If the eggpuff is not done, return it to the oven for a few more minutes.

steps.title: Serve the Eggpuff
steps.instruction: Remove the eggpuff from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes. Serve warm or at room temperature, sliced into thick wedges.
steps.why: This step allows the eggpuff to cool and sets the filling.
steps.tip: Serve the eggpuff with a side of fruit or whipped cream for a sweet treat.

name: Gulab Jamun
ingredients: 1 cup gulab syrup (see below for recipe)

steps.title: Make the dough
steps.instruction: In a large mixing bowl, combine the paneer, flour, baking soda, cardamom powder and salt. Mix well until the dough comes together.
steps.why: This dough will help us create the jamun.
steps.tip: Make sure the paneer is crumbly and the dough is not too sticky.

steps.title: Add ghee and milk
steps.instruction: Add the ghee or oil and lukewarm milk to the dough. Mix well until the dough is smooth and pliable.
steps.why: The ghee and milk will help the dough to come together and give it a rich flavor.
steps.tip: Do not over-mix the dough.

steps.title: Knead the dough
steps.instruction: Knead the dough for 10-15 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
steps.why: This will help to develop the gluten in the dough and make it easier to shape.
steps.tip: Use a standing mixer or a food processor to knead the dough if possible.

steps.title: Rest the dough
steps.instruction: Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 30 minutes.
steps.why: This will allow the yeast to activate and the dough to rise.
steps.tip: Make sure the dough is in a warm place while it rests.

steps.title: Make the khoya
steps.instruction: Combine the dried milk and a little water to make a smooth paste. Set aside.
steps.why: The khoya will add a rich and creamy texture to the jamun.
steps.tip: You can also use store-bought khoya if you prefer.

steps.title: Shape the dough
steps.instruction: Take small balls of the dough and flatten them slightly into a disk shape. Then wrap each disk around a small ball of khoya to make a jamun shape.
steps.why: This will give the jamun its characteristic shape and texture.
steps.tip: Make sure the dough is not too thick or too thin.

steps.title: Fry the jamun
steps.instruction: Heat enough oil in a deep frying pan to deep-fry the jamun. When the oil is hot, add the jamun and fry until they are golden brown.
steps.why: The frying will give the jamun a crispy exterior and a soft interior.
steps.tip: Do not overcrowd the pan and fry the jamun in batches if necessary.

steps.title: Soak in gulab syrup
steps.instruction: Once the jamun are fried, soak them in the warm gulab syrup for at least 30 minutes before serving.
steps.why: The gulab syrup will give the jamun its characteristic sweet and fragrant flavor.
steps.tip: Make sure the syrup is warm and the jamun are fully soaked before serving.

name: Chicken Biryani
ingredients: 500g boneless chicken thighs, cut into 1 inch pieces
1 cup mixed basmati rice (separately rinsed and drained)
1 cup whole wheat bread slices (dried and crumbled)
2 tbsp saffron threads, soaked in 2 tbsp hot water

steps.title: Heat Oil and Sauté Onions & Spices
steps.instruction: In a large heavy bottom pan, heat 4 tbsp of vegetable oil and ghee over medium heat. Add cumin seeds, cloves, cinnamon stick, green chilies, and sauté for 1 minute. Add thinly sliced onions and fry until they turn golden brown.
steps.why: This creates the base of our biryani and brings out the flavors of the spices.
steps.tip: Use a mixture of both ghee and oil for an authentic flavor.

steps.title: Marinate Chicken
steps.instruction: In a separate bowl, combine marinated chicken, 1 tsp cumin powder, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, and salt to taste. Mix well and keep aside for 30 minutes.
steps.why: This process tenderizes the chicken and brings out its flavor.
steps.tip: Use yogurt instead of ghee for marinating chicken for extra tenderness.

steps.title: Fry Marinated Chicken
steps.instruction: After 30 minutes, heat 2 additional tbsp of oil in the same pan. Remove the chicken from marinade and add to the pan. Cook until the chicken turns golden brown and is cooked through.
steps.why: This adds texture and flavor to the biryani.
steps.tip: Don't overcook the chicken, it should be juicy.

steps.title: Prepare Basmati Rice
steps.instruction: Rinse the basmati rice in a fine mesh sieve until the water runs clear. Drain the water and add 2 cups of water. Add 1 tsp of salt to the water and allow it to soak for 20 minutes.
steps.why: Soaking basmati rice makes it easier to cook and gives it a better texture.
steps.tip: Don't over-soak, it should just absorb some water.

steps.title: Cook Basmati Rice
steps.instruction: After 20 minutes, drain the water from the rice and transfer it to a large pan. Add more water if needed, and bring it to a boil. Reduce the heat to medium-low, cover the pan, and simmer until it's cooked.
steps.why: This cooks the rice to perfection and keeps its grain texture.
steps.tip: Use a pressure cooker to speed up the cooking process.

steps.title: Layer Chicken and Rice
steps.instruction: Once the basmati rice and chicken are cooked, layer them in a large pot or Dutch oven. Start with a layer of rice, then a layer of chicken, sprinkling some fried onions and coriander leaves in between each layer.
steps.why: This creates the iconic layered structure of the biryani.
steps.tip: Use a mixture of basmati and other long grain rice for extra texture.

steps.title: Steam the Biryani
steps.instruction: Close the pot with a tight-fitting lid and let it steam on low heat for 15-20 minutes. This helps to infuse the flavors of the spices into the rice and chicken.
steps.why: Steaming brings out the hidden flavors and adds texture to the biryani.
steps.tip: You can also use a bamboo steamer for added flavor and texture.

steps.title: Serve Hot
steps.instruction: Finally, open the lid and gently mix the rice and chicken together. Garnish with chopped fresh coriander leaves and a sprinkle of saffron water. Serve hot and enjoy!
steps.why: This adds the final flourish to the dish and makes it presentable.
steps.tip: Serve with a side of yogurt or raita to balance the spice levels.

steps.title: Prepare the Marinade
steps.instruction: In a bowl, mix together 1/2 cup of gram flour, 1 tsp of ginger powder, salt, and black pepper. Add the chicken pieces and mix well to coat evenly. Let it marinate for at least 15 minutes.
steps.why: The marinade helps to tenderize the chicken and adds flavor to the dish.
steps.tip: You can adjust the amount of ginger powder and chilli sauce according to your taste.

steps.title: Make the Sauce
steps.instruction: In a small bowl, combine 2 tbsp of soy sauce, 1 tsp of vinegar, 1 tsp of tomato ketchup, and 2 tsp of chilli sauce. Mix well.
steps.why: The sauce adds a sweet and tangy flavor to the dish.
steps.tip: You can adjust the amount of chilli sauce according to your taste.

steps.title: Cook the Chicken
steps.instruction: Heat 1 tsp of vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat. Remove the chicken from the marinade and cook until it's golden brown and cooked through.
steps.why: Cooking the chicken in a pan helps to crisp the outside and keep the inside tender.
steps.tip: Don't overcook the chicken.

steps.title: Make the Garnish
steps.instruction: Heat 1 tsp of butter in a pan over medium heat. Add 2 cloves of minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
steps.why: The garlic adds flavor to the dish and helps to balance the sweetness of the sauce.
steps.tip: Use high-quality garlic for the best flavor.

steps.title: Mix the Sauce and Gravy
steps.instruction: Add 2 tbsp of soy sauce, 1 tsp of vinegar, and 2 tsp of tomato ketchup to the pan with the garlic. Mix well to combine.
steps.why: The sauce and gravy help to moisten the dish and add flavor.
steps.tip: You can adjust the amount of soy sauce and chilli sauce according to your taste.

steps.title: Add the Chopped Vegetables
steps.instruction: Add 1 small onion and 2 small tomatoes to the pan and sauté until they're tender.
steps.why: The vegetables add texture and flavor to the dish.
steps.tip: Use high-quality vegetables for the best flavor.

steps.title: Add the Sauce and Chicken
steps.instruction: Add the prepared sauce and the cooked chicken to the pan with the vegetables. Mix well to combine.
steps.why: The sauce and chicken help to moisten the dish and add flavor.
steps.tip: You can adjust the amount of sauce according to your taste.

steps.title: Serve and Garnish
steps.instruction: Transfer the Chicken Manchurian to a serving platter and garnish with 2 tbsp of chopped scallions. Serve immediately.
steps.why: The scallions add flavor and texture to the dish.
steps.tip: Use fresh scallions for the best flavor.

name: Fish Fry
ingredients: 1 pound (450g) cod or haddock fillets, cut into 1-inch (2.5 cm) pieces

steps.title: Prepare the coating
steps.instruction: In a shallow dish, mix together the flour, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper.
steps.why: A good coating is essential for a crispy fish fry.
steps.tip: Use a combination of flour and spices for a more flavorful coating.

steps.title: Prepare the buttermilk
steps.instruction: In a separate shallow dish, whisk together the buttermilk and eggs.
steps.why: Soaking the fish in buttermilk helps to tenderize it and creates a crispy coating.
steps.tip: Make sure to mix the buttermilk and eggs well before adding the fish.

steps.title: Prepare the breadcrumbs
steps.instruction: In a third shallow dish, place the panko breadcrumbs.
steps.why: Panko breadcrumbs provide a light and crispy coating.
steps.tip: Use fresh breadcrumbs for the best results.

steps.title: Dredge the fish in flour
steps.instruction: Dredge the fish pieces in the flour mixture, shaking off any excess.
steps.why: Coating the fish in flour helps the breading stick.
steps.tip: Don't overcoat the fish with flour, or the breading may not stick well.

steps.title: Soak the fish in buttermilk
steps.instruction: Soak the floured fish pieces in the buttermilk mixture for at least 30 minutes.
steps.why: The acidity in the buttermilk helps to break down the proteins in the fish, making it tender and moist.
steps.tip: Make sure the fish is fully submerged in the buttermilk mixture.

steps.title: Dredge the fish in breadcrumbs
steps.instruction: Remove the fish from the buttermilk mixture, letting any excess liquid drip off.
steps.why: A good coating is essential for a crispy fish fry.
steps.tip: Press the breadcrumbs gently onto the fish to ensure they stick.

steps.title: Heat the oil
steps.instruction: Heat about 1/2-inch (1 cm) of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F (180°C).
steps.why: The oil needs to be hot enough to fry the fish quickly and get a crispy coating.
steps.tip: Use a thermometer to ensure the oil has reached the correct temperature.

steps.title: Fry the fish
steps.instruction: Carefully place the breaded fish pieces in the hot oil and fry for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and cooked through.
steps.why: Frying the fish in hot oil creates a crispy and golden-brown coating.
steps.tip: Don't overcrowd the skillet, fry the fish in batches if necessary.
